Summarized the proactive-context product spec: a local-first RAG system for markdown files with commands like init, query, generate, and stats, using local embeddings and sqlite-vec storage.

here's what I'm thinking: what if we were to also index everything the user ever says (this "generate" tool will be called every time I use claude code in any project)

what if we keep track of every conversation the user is participating and we start learning lessons and guarding whatever the user is saying and we include that information in whatever the user is saying when we inject context proactively? thoughts?

Thought through the idea, identifying two sub-problems: conversation capture/indexing (straightforward extension) and lesson extraction/proactive injection (harder, more valuable). Suggested using Claude Code hooks for injection and a separate corpus for conversations, while flagging risks like stale lessons and cross-contamination.
add a justfile that installs it in my ~/.bin or ~/bin or something like that that's in my $PATH and run it
Done — `proactive-context` is now in `~/.bin/` and ready to use. The justfile has a single `install` recipe for rebuilding and redeploying.
is there a way to see ingestion in real time? like a stats --watch
No --watch flag exists yet; you can approximate it with `watch -n 1 proactive-context stats`, or the agent offered to implement a proper live stats --watch feature.
add it -- make it look good and colorize stats too
Added colorized stats output with daemon status indicators, file/chunk counts, DB size, and embedding info, plus a --watch flag that refreshes in-place every second.
Done — `~/.bin/proactive-context` is up to date and `~/.bin` is already in your `$PATH`.
